[[JA:美多彌神社]] {{wikidata link|Q276831}} == Japanese content == {{神社 | 名称 = Mitami Shrine | 画像 = [[File:Mitami-jinja2.jpg|270px]]
Torii and haiden | 所在地 = {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Sakai|Q193428|WD=Q193428|1=Sakai}}{{ill|lt=Minami Ward (Sakai)|Q1041996|WD=Q1041996|lt=Minami Ward|1=Minami-ku, Sakai}}Kamotani-dai 1-49-1 | 位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|28|30|N|135|29|08|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | 祭神 = {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|Q2564508|WD=Q2564508|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Koyane}} | 社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small)
former {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} | 創建 = Unknown | 本殿 = {{ill|lt=irimoya-zukuri|Q855025|WD=Q855025|1=East Asian hip-and-gable roof}} | 例祭 = First Sunday of October | 神事 = {{ill|lt=yabusame|Q1886157|WD=Q1886157|1=Yabusame}} festival | 地図 = {{Location map|Japan Osaka|lat_deg=34|lat_min=28|lat_sec=30|lon_deg=135|lon_min=29|lon_sec=8|label=Mitami Shrine|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|28|30|N|135|29|08|E}}}} }} '''Mitami Shrine''' (みたみじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Minami Ward (Sakai)|Q1041996|WD=Q1041996|lt=Minami Ward|1=Minami-ku, Sakai}}, {{ill|lt=Sakai|Q193428|WD=Q193428|1=Sakai}}, {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and under the former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} system it was a {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. The grove of {{ill|lt=Shiribukagashi|Q149476|WD=Q149476|1=Lithocarpus glaber}} in the precincts is selected as one of the {{ill|lt=100 Green Sites of Osaka|Q11439936|WD=Q11439936|1=Ōsaka Midori no Hyakusen}}. === Enshrined deities === * Principal deity - {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|Q2564508|WD=Q2564508|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Koyane}} * Jointly enshrined deities - {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|WD=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi|Q10936701|WD=Q10936701|1=Ōmononushi}}-no-Mikoto, {{ill|lt=Mikumari deity|Q11129547|WD=Q11129547|lt=Ame-no-Mikumari-no-Ōkami|1=Mikumari}}, {{ill|lt=Ichikishimahime|Q11059454|WD=Q11059454|WD=Q11059454|lt=Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|1=Ichikishima-hime}}, {{ill|lt=Emperor Ojin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|lt=Homuda-wake-no-Mikoto|1=Emperor Ōjin}}, {{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005|1=Sugawara no Michizane}}, {{ill|lt=Izanagi|Q813858|WD=Q813858|lt=Izanagi-no-Ōkami|1=Izanagi}}, {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|1=Ōkuninushi}}-no-Mikoto === History === The exact founding date is unknown, but the name "Mitami Shrine" appears in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} completed in {{ill|lt=Engi|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|lt=Engi|1=Enchō}} 5 ({{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470|1=927}})[https://www.mitami-shrine.com/about/ Mitami Shrine homepage: About Mitami Shrine]. The origin of the shrine name is said to come from "Mitami no Atai" recorded in ''Shinsen Shōjiroku'' and the "Kamiwake entry of Izumi Province." It states that the Mitami no Atai clan were descendants of {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|Q2564508|WD=Q2564508|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Koyane}}, and that the reading of "mitami" is corrected as "mitami" written 美多彌. In the era of {{ill|lt=Emperor Go-Daigo|Q313942|WD=Q313942|1=Emperor Go-Daigo}} during the {{ill|lt=Nanboku-chō period|Q264012|WD=Q264012|1=Nanboku-chō period}}, the shrine is said to have had an extensive precinct of 30,000 tsubo and magnificent buildings. It was also revered as a guardian deity by {{ill|lt=Kusunoki Masashige|Q382234|WD=Q382234|1=Kusunoki Masashige}}. In the 1500s, the shrine-temple Tokusen-ji ({{ill|lt=jingūji|Q1074971|WD=Q1074971|1=Jingū-ji}}) was built in the precincts, and shrine priests and temple clerics managed shrine affairs. It is also said to have been protected by {{ill|lt=Oda Nobunaga|Q171411|WD=Q171411|1=Oda Nobunaga}}, and thus adopted the Oda clan crest (five-leaf mokkō, {{ill|lt=mokkō crest|Q11519285|WD=Q11519285|1=Mokkō mon}}) as its shrine crestMitami Shrine history text. However, in {{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} 5 ({{ill|lt=1577|Q6704|WD=Q6704|1=1577}}), it was burned in war during Nobunaga's attack on Saika in Kii ({{ill|lt=Kishū campaign|Q10855721|WD=Q10855721|1=Battle of Kishuu}}), together with nearby {{ill|lt=Hōkō-ji (Sakai)|Q65269233|WD=Q65269233|lt=Hōkō-ji|1=Hōkō-ji}}. In {{ill|lt=Bunroku|Q1373039|WD=Q1373039|1=Bunroku}} 1 ({{ill|lt=1592|Q6769|WD=Q6769|1=1592}}), the honden was rebuilt by {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Wada Dōsan|1=UNKNOWN}} of the {{ill|lt=Kusunoki clan|Q11541208|WD=Q11541208|1=Kusunoki clan}}, and the shrine name was changed to Gozu Tennō-sha[https://www.mitami-shrine.com/yabusame/ Mitami Shrine homepage: About Yabusame]. During the {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} separation of Shinto and Buddhism ({{ill|lt=shinbutsu bunri|Q1970535|WD=Q1970535|1=Shinbutsu bunri}}), Tokusen-ji was abolished and the shrine renamed Yasaka Shrine. It was ranked as a {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. In {{ill|lt=1907|Q2048|WD=Q2048|1=1907}} (Meiji 40), five nearby village shrines and three unranked shrines were merged, and it was renamed Mitami Shrine as listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}. On March 29, {{ill|lt=2014|Q1999|WD=Q1999|1=2014}} ({{ill|lt=Heisei|Q234510|WD=Q234510|1=Heisei era}} 26), the {{ill|lt=yabusame|Q1886157|WD=Q1886157|1=Yabusame}} rite was revived. The shrine grove covers 8,250 {{ill|lt=square meters|Q25343|WD=Q25343|lt=m2|1=Square metre}} and includes evergreen broadleaf trees such as {{ill|lt=Shiribukagashi|Q149476|WD=Q149476|1=Lithocarpus glaber}}, {{ill|lt=Arakashi|Q714124|WD=Q714124|1=Quercus glauca}}, {{ill|lt=sakaki|Q775470|WD=Q775470|1=Cleyera japonica}}, and camphor. Shiribukagashi in particular rarely forms a forest; because this grove lies near the eastern limit of its distribution, it was designated an Osaka Prefecture {{ill|lt=natural monument|Q23790|WD=Q23790|1=Natural monument}} in {{ill|lt=1973|Q2477|WD=Q2477|1=1973}} ({{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 48). The shiribukagashi grove is also selected as one of the {{ill|lt=100 Green Sites of Osaka|Q11439936|WD=Q11439936|1=Ōsaka Midori no Hyakusen}}{{Cite web|和書|url= http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/midorikikaku/toshiryokka/hyakusenn.html|title=100 Green Sites of Osaka|publisher=Osaka Prefecture |accessdate=2016-12-23}}. === Precincts === * Honden - rebuilt in the {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} period. * Heiden - rebuilt in {{ill|lt=1972|Q2476|WD=Q2476|1=1972}} ({{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 47). * Haiden - rebuilt in 1972 (Shōwa 47). * Shrine office * Bishūkan === Subsidiary shrines === * Kotohira-sha * Hakuryū Tennō-sha === Cultural properties === === Osaka Prefecture designated natural monument === * Mitami Shrine {{ill|lt=Shiribukagashi|Q149476|WD=Q149476|lt=shiribukagashi|1=Lithocarpus glaber}} shrine grove === Location === * Kamotani-dai 1-49-1, Minami Ward, Sakai, Osaka Prefecture === Access === * {{ill|lt=Nankai Senboku Line|Q2758012|WD=Q2758012|1=Semboku Line}} {{ill|lt=Kōmyōike Station|Q6455215|WD=Q6455215|1=Kōmyōike Station}} - 20 minutes on foot === Footnotes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} === Notes === {{Notelist2}} === Sources === {{Reflist|2}} === Related items === * {{ill|lt=Izumi Wada clan|Q65252203|WD=Q65252203|1=Izumi Wada clan}} === External links === {{commons|Category:Mitami-jinja}} *[http://mitami-shrine.com/ Mitami Shrine] *[https://www.sakai-tcb.or.jp/spot/detail/205 Mitami Shrine - Sakai Tourism Guide] {{大阪みどりの百選}} {{神道 横}} {{Shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Mitami Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Mitami Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Mitami Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izumi Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Sakai, Osaka]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izumi Province]] [[Category:Son-sha in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:堺市南区の歴史]] [[Category:堺市南区の建築物]] [[Category:泉北ニュータウン]] [[Category:Natural Monuments Designated by Osaka Prefecture]][[Category:qq]]

former {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} | 創建 = Unknown | 本殿 = {{ill|lt=irimoya-zukuri|Q855025|WD=Q855025|1=East Asian hip-and-gable roof}} | 例祭 = First Sunday of October | 神事 = {{ill|lt=yabusame|Q1886157|WD=Q1886157|1=Yabusame}} festival | 地図 = {{Location map|Japan Osaka|lat_deg=34|lat_min=28|lat_sec=30|lon_deg=135|lon_min=29|lon_sec=8|label=Mitami Shrine|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|28|30|N|135|29|08|E}}}} }} '''Mitami Shrine''' (みたみじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Minami Ward (Sakai)|Q1041996|WD=Q1041996|lt=Minami Ward|1=Minami-ku, Sakai}}, {{ill|lt=Sakai|Q193428|WD=Q193428|1=Sakai}}, {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and under the former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} system it was a {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. The grove of {{ill|lt=Shiribukagashi|Q149476|WD=Q149476|1=Lithocarpus glaber}} in the precincts is selected as one of the {{ill|lt=100 Green Sites of Osaka|Q11439936|WD=Q11439936|1=Ōsaka Midori no Hyakusen}}. === Enshrined deities === * Principal deity - {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|Q2564508|WD=Q2564508|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Koyane}} * Jointly enshrined deities - {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|WD=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi|Q10936701|WD=Q10936701|1=Ōmononushi}}-no-Mikoto, {{ill|lt=Mikumari deity|Q11129547|WD=Q11129547|lt=Ame-no-Mikumari-no-Ōkami|1=Mikumari}}, {{ill|lt=Ichikishimahime|Q11059454|WD=Q11059454|WD=Q11059454|lt=Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|1=Ichikishima-hime}}, {{ill|lt=Emperor Ojin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|lt=Homuda-wake-no-Mikoto|1=Emperor Ōjin}}, {{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005|1=Sugawara no Michizane}}, {{ill|lt=Izanagi|Q813858|WD=Q813858|lt=Izanagi-no-Ōkami|1=Izanagi}}, {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|1=Ōkuninushi}}-no-Mikoto === History === The exact founding date is unknown, but the name "Mitami Shrine" appears in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} completed in {{ill|lt=Engi|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|lt=Engi|1=Enchō}} 5 ({{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470|1=927}})[https://www.mitami-shrine.com/about/ Mitami Shrine homepage: About Mitami Shrine]. The origin of the shrine name is said to come from "Mitami no Atai" recorded in ''Shinsen Shōjiroku'' and the "Kamiwake entry of Izumi Province." It states that the Mitami no Atai clan were descendants of {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|Q2564508|WD=Q2564508|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Koyane}}, and that the reading of "mitami" is corrected as "mitami" written 美多彌. In the era of {{ill|lt=Emperor Go-Daigo|Q313942|WD=Q313942|1=Emperor Go-Daigo}} during the {{ill|lt=Nanboku-chō period|Q264012|WD=Q264012|1=Nanboku-chō period}}, the shrine is said to have had an extensive precinct of 30,000 tsubo and magnificent buildings. It was also revered as a guardian deity by {{ill|lt=Kusunoki Masashige|Q382234|WD=Q382234|1=Kusunoki Masashige}}. In the 1500s, the shrine-temple Tokusen-ji ({{ill|lt=jingūji|Q1074971|WD=Q1074971|1=Jingū-ji}}) was built in the precincts, and shrine priests and temple clerics managed shrine affairs. It is also said to have been protected by {{ill|lt=Oda Nobunaga|Q171411|WD=Q171411|1=Oda Nobunaga}}, and thus adopted the Oda clan crest (five-leaf mokkō, {{ill|lt=mokkō crest|Q11519285|WD=Q11519285|1=Mokkō mon}}) as its shrine crestMitami Shrine history text. However, in {{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} 5 ({{ill|lt=1577|Q6704|WD=Q6704|1=1577}}), it was burned in war during Nobunaga's attack on Saika in Kii ({{ill|lt=Kishū campaign|Q10855721|WD=Q10855721|1=Battle of Kishuu}}), together with nearby {{ill|lt=Hōkō-ji (Sakai)|Q65269233|WD=Q65269233|lt=Hōkō-ji|1=Hōkō-ji}}. In {{ill|lt=Bunroku|Q1373039|WD=Q1373039|1=Bunroku}} 1 ({{ill|lt=1592|Q6769|WD=Q6769|1=1592}}), the honden was rebuilt by {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Wada Dōsan|1=UNKNOWN}} of the {{ill|lt=Kusunoki clan|Q11541208|WD=Q11541208|1=Kusunoki clan}}, and the shrine name was changed to Gozu Tennō-sha[https://www.mitami-shrine.com/yabusame/ Mitami Shrine homepage: About Yabusame]. During the {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} separation of Shinto and Buddhism ({{ill|lt=shinbutsu bunri|Q1970535|WD=Q1970535|1=Shinbutsu bunri}}), Tokusen-ji was abolished and the shrine renamed Yasaka Shrine. It was ranked as a {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. In {{ill|lt=1907|Q2048|WD=Q2048|1=1907}} (Meiji 40), five nearby village shrines and three unranked shrines were merged, and it was renamed Mitami Shrine as listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}. On March 29, {{ill|lt=2014|Q1999|WD=Q1999|1=2014}} ({{ill|lt=Heisei|Q234510|WD=Q234510|1=Heisei era}} 26), the {{ill|lt=yabusame|Q1886157|WD=Q1886157|1=Yabusame}} rite was revived. The shrine grove covers 8,250 {{ill|lt=square meters|Q25343|WD=Q25343|lt=m2|1=Square metre}} and includes evergreen broadleaf trees such as {{ill|lt=Shiribukagashi|Q149476|WD=Q149476|1=Lithocarpus glaber}}, {{ill|lt=Arakashi|Q714124|WD=Q714124|1=Quercus glauca}}, {{ill|lt=sakaki|Q775470|WD=Q775470|1=Cleyera japonica}}, and camphor. Shiribukagashi in particular rarely forms a forest; because this grove lies near the eastern limit of its distribution, it was designated an Osaka Prefecture {{ill|lt=natural monument|Q23790|WD=Q23790|1=Natural monument}} in {{ill|lt=1973|Q2477|WD=Q2477|1=1973}} ({{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 48). The shiribukagashi grove is also selected as one of the {{ill|lt=100 Green Sites of Osaka|Q11439936|WD=Q11439936|1=Ōsaka Midori no Hyakusen}}{{Cite web|和書|url= http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/midorikikaku/toshiryokka/hyakusenn.html|title=100 Green Sites of Osaka|publisher=Osaka Prefecture |accessdate=2016-12-23}}. === Precincts === * Honden - rebuilt in the {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} period. * Heiden - rebuilt in {{ill|lt=1972|Q2476|WD=Q2476|1=1972}} ({{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 47). * Haiden - rebuilt in 1972 (Shōwa 47). * Shrine office * Bishūkan === Subsidiary shrines === * Kotohira-sha * Hakuryū Tennō-sha === Cultural properties === === Osaka Prefecture designated natural monument === * Mitami Shrine {{ill|lt=Shiribukagashi|Q149476|WD=Q149476|lt=shiribukagashi|1=Lithocarpus glaber}} shrine grove === Location === * Kamotani-dai 1-49-1, Minami Ward, Sakai, Osaka Prefecture === Access === * {{ill|lt=Nankai Senboku Line|Q2758012|WD=Q2758012|1=Semboku Line}} {{ill|lt=Kōmyōike Station|Q6455215|WD=Q6455215|1=Kōmyōike Station}} - 20 minutes on foot === Footnotes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} === Notes === {{Notelist2}} === Sources === {{Reflist|2}} === Related items === * {{ill|lt=Izumi Wada clan|Q65252203|WD=Q65252203|1=Izumi Wada clan}} === External links === {{commons|Category:Mitami-jinja}} *[http://mitami-shrine.com/ Mitami Shrine] *[https://www.sakai-tcb.or.jp/spot/detail/205 Mitami Shrine - Sakai Tourism Guide] {{大阪みどりの百選}} {{神道 横}} {{Shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Mitami Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Mitami Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Mitami Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izumi Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Sakai, Osaka]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izumi Province]] [[Category:Son-sha in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:堺市南区の歴史]] [[Category:堺市南区の建築物]] [[Category:泉北ニュータウン]] [[Category:Natural Monuments Designated by Osaka Prefecture]]

In {{ill|lt=Bunroku|Q1373039|WD=Q1373039|1=Bunroku}} 1 ({{ill|lt=1592|Q6769|WD=Q6769|1=1592}}), the honden was rebuilt by {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Wada Dōsan|1=UNKNOWN}} of the {{ill|lt=Kusunoki clan|Q11541208|WD=Q11541208|1=Kusunoki clan}}, and the shrine name was changed to Gozu Tennō-sha[https://www.mitami-shrine.com/yabusame/ Mitami Shrine homepage: About Yabusame]. During the {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} separation of Shinto and Buddhism ({{ill|lt=shinbutsu bunri|Q1970535|WD=Q1970535|1=Shinbutsu bunri}}), Tokusen-ji was abolished and the shrine renamed Yasaka Shrine. It was ranked as a {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. In {{ill|lt=1907|Q2048|WD=Q2048|1=1907}} (Meiji 40), five nearby village shrines and three unranked shrines were merged, and it was renamed Mitami Shrine as listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}. On March 29, {{ill|lt=2014|Q1999|WD=Q1999|1=2014}} ({{ill|lt=Heisei|Q234510|WD=Q234510|1=Heisei era}} 26), the {{ill|lt=yabusame|Q1886157|WD=Q1886157|1=Yabusame}} rite was revived. The shrine grove covers 8,250 {{ill|lt=square meters|Q25343|WD=Q25343|lt=m2|1=Square metre}} and includes evergreen broadleaf trees such as {{ill|lt=Shiribukagashi|Q149476|WD=Q149476|1=Lithocarpus glaber}}, {{ill|lt=Arakashi|Q714124|WD=Q714124|1=Quercus glauca}}, {{ill|lt=sakaki|Q775470|WD=Q775470|1=Cleyera japonica}}, and camphor. Shiribukagashi in particular rarely forms a forest; because this grove lies near the eastern limit of its distribution, it was designated an Osaka Prefecture {{ill|lt=natural monument|Q23790|WD=Q23790|1=Natural monument}} in {{ill|lt=1973|Q2477|WD=Q2477|1=1973}} ({{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 48). The shiribukagashi grove is also selected as one of the {{ill|lt=100 Green Sites of Osaka|Q11439936|WD=Q11439936|1=Ōsaka Midori no Hyakusen}}{{Cite web|和書|url= http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/midorikikaku/toshiryokka/hyakusenn.html|title=100 Green Sites of Osaka|publisher=Osaka Prefecture |accessdate=2016-12-23}}. === Precincts === * Honden - rebuilt in the {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} period. * Heiden - rebuilt in {{ill|lt=1972|Q2476|WD=Q2476|1=1972}} ({{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 47). * Haiden - rebuilt in 1972 (Shōwa 47). * Shrine office * Bishūkan === Subsidiary shrines === * Kotohira-sha * Hakuryū Tennō-sha === Cultural properties === === Osaka Prefecture designated natural monument === * Mitami Shrine {{ill|lt=Shiribukagashi|Q149476|WD=Q149476|lt=shiribukagashi|1=Lithocarpus glaber}} shrine grove === Location === * Kamotani-dai 1-49-1, Minami Ward, Sakai, Osaka Prefecture === Access === * {{ill|lt=Nankai Senboku Line|Q2758012|WD=Q2758012|1=Semboku Line}} {{ill|lt=Kōmyōike Station|Q6455215|WD=Q6455215|1=Kōmyōike Station}} - 20 minutes on foot === Footnotes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} === Notes === {{Notelist2}} === Sources === {{Reflist|2}} === Related items === * {{ill|lt=Izumi Wada clan|Q65252203|WD=Q65252203|1=Izumi Wada clan}} === External links === {{commons|Category:Mitami-jinja}} *[http://mitami-shrine.com/ Mitami Shrine] *[https://www.sakai-tcb.or.jp/spot/detail/205 Mitami Shrine - Sakai Tourism Guide] {{大阪みどりの百選}} {{神道 横}} {{Shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Mitami Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Mitami Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Mitami Shrine]][[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izumi Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Sakai, Osaka]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izumi Province]] [[Category:Son-sha in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:堺市南区の歴史]] [[Category:堺市南区の建築物]] [[Category:泉北ニュータウン]] [[Category:Natural Monuments Designated by Osaka Prefecture]]

[[JA:美多彌神社]] {{wikidata link|Q276831}} == Japanese content == {{神社 | 名称 = Mitami Shrine | 画像 = [[File:Mitami-jinja2.jpg|270px]]
Torii and haiden | 所在地 = {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Sakai|Q193428|WD=Q193428|1=Sakai}}{{ill|lt=Minami Ward (Sakai)|Q1041996|WD=Q1041996|lt=Minami Ward|1=Minami-ku, Sakai}}Kamotani-dai 1-49-1 | 位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|28|30|N|135|29|08|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | 祭神 = {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|Q2564508|WD=Q2564508|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Koyane}} | 社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small)
former {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} | 創建 = Unknown | 本殿 = {{ill|lt=irimoya-zukuri|Q855025|WD=Q855025|1=East Asian hip-and-gable roof}} | 例祭 = First Sunday of October | 神事 = {{ill|lt=yabusame|Q1886157|WD=Q1886157|1=Yabusame}} festival | 地図 = {{Location map|Japan Osaka|lat_deg=34|lat_min=28|lat_sec=30|lon_deg=135|lon_min=29|lon_sec=8|label=Mitami Shrine|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|28|30|N|135|29|08|E}}}} }} '''Mitami Shrine''' (みたみじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Minami Ward (Sakai)|Q1041996|WD=Q1041996|lt=Minami Ward|1=Minami-ku, Sakai}}, {{ill|lt=Sakai|Q193428|WD=Q193428|1=Sakai}}, {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and under the former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} system it was a {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. The grove of {{ill|lt=Shiribukagashi|Q149476|WD=Q149476|1=Lithocarpus glaber}} in the precincts is selected as one of the {{ill|lt=100 Green Sites of Osaka|Q11439936|WD=Q11439936|1=Ōsaka Midori no Hyakusen}}. === Enshrined deities === * Principal deity - {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|Q2564508|WD=Q2564508|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Koyane}} * Jointly enshrined deities - {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|WD=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi|Q10936701|WD=Q10936701|1=Ōmononushi}}-no-Mikoto, {{ill|lt=Mikumari deity|Q11129547|WD=Q11129547|lt=Ame-no-Mikumari-no-Ōkami|1=Mikumari}}, {{ill|lt=Ichikishimahime|Q11059454|WD=Q11059454|WD=Q11059454|lt=Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|1=Ichikishima-hime}}, {{ill|lt=Emperor Ojin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|lt=Homuda-wake-no-Mikoto|1=Emperor Ōjin}}, {{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005|1=Sugawara no Michizane}}, {{ill|lt=Izanagi|Q813858|WD=Q813858|lt=Izanagi-no-Ōkami|1=Izanagi}}, {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|1=Ōkuninushi}}-no-Mikoto === History === The exact founding date is unknown, but the name "Mitami Shrine" appears in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} completed in {{ill|lt=Engi|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|lt=Engi|1=Enchō}} 5 ({{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470|1=927}})[https://www.mitami-shrine.com/about/ Mitami Shrine homepage: About Mitami Shrine]. The origin of the shrine name is said to come from "Mitami no Atai" recorded in ''Shinsen Shōjiroku'' and the "Kamiwake entry of Izumi Province." It states that the Mitami no Atai clan were descendants of {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|Q2564508|WD=Q2564508|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Koyane}}, and that the reading of "mitami" is corrected as "mitami" written 美多彌. In the era of {{ill|lt=Emperor Go-Daigo|Q313942|WD=Q313942|1=Emperor Go-Daigo}} during the {{ill|lt=Nanboku-chō period|Q264012|WD=Q264012|1=Nanboku-chō period}}, the shrine is said to have had an extensive precinct of 30,000 tsubo and magnificent buildings. It was also revered as a guardian deity by {{ill|lt=Kusunoki Masashige|Q382234|WD=Q382234|1=Kusunoki Masashige}}. In the 1500s, the shrine-temple Tokusen-ji ({{ill|lt=jingūji|Q1074971|WD=Q1074971|1=Jingū-ji}}) was built in the precincts, and shrine priests and temple clerics managed shrine affairs. It is also said to have been protected by {{ill|lt=Oda Nobunaga|Q171411|WD=Q171411|1=Oda Nobunaga}}, and thus adopted the Oda clan crest (five-leaf mokkō, {{ill|lt=mokkō crest|Q11519285|WD=Q11519285|1=Mokkō mon}}) as its shrine crestMitami Shrine history text. However, in {{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} 5 ({{ill|lt=1577|Q6704|WD=Q6704|1=1577}}), it was burned in war during Nobunaga's attack on Saika in Kii ({{ill|lt=Kishū campaign|Q10855721|WD=Q10855721|1=Battle of Kishuu}}), together with nearby {{ill|lt=Hōkō-ji (Sakai)|Q65269233|WD=Q65269233|lt=Hōkō-ji|1=Hōkō-ji}}. In {{ill|lt=Bunroku|Q1373039|WD=Q1373039|1=Bunroku}} 1 ({{ill|lt=1592|Q6769|WD=Q6769|1=1592}}), the honden was rebuilt by {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Wada Dōsan|1=UNKNOWN}} of the {{ill|lt=Kusunoki clan|Q11541208|WD=Q11541208|1=Kusunoki clan}}, and the shrine name was changed to Gozu Tennō-sha[https://www.mitami-shrine.com/yabusame/ Mitami Shrine homepage: About Yabusame]. During the {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} separation of Shinto and Buddhism ({{ill|lt=shinbutsu bunri|Q1970535|WD=Q1970535|1=Shinbutsu bunri}}), Tokusen-ji was abolished and the shrine renamed Yasaka Shrine. It was ranked as a {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. In {{ill|lt=1907|Q2048|WD=Q2048|1=1907}} (Meiji 40), five nearby village shrines and three unranked shrines were merged, and it was renamed Mitami Shrine as listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}. On March 29, {{ill|lt=2014|Q1999|WD=Q1999|1=2014}} ({{ill|lt=Heisei|Q234510|WD=Q234510|1=Heisei era}} 26), the {{ill|lt=yabusame|Q1886157|WD=Q1886157|1=Yabusame}} rite was revived. The shrine grove covers 8,250 {{ill|lt=square meters|Q25343|WD=Q25343|lt=m2|1=Square metre}} and includes evergreen broadleaf trees such as {{ill|lt=Shiribukagashi|Q149476|WD=Q149476|1=Lithocarpus glaber}}, {{ill|lt=Arakashi|Q714124|WD=Q714124|1=Quercus glauca}}, {{ill|lt=sakaki|Q775470|WD=Q775470|1=Cleyera japonica}}, and camphor. Shiribukagashi in particular rarely forms a forest; because this grove lies near the eastern limit of its distribution, it was designated an Osaka Prefecture {{ill|lt=natural monument|Q23790|WD=Q23790|1=Natural monument}} in {{ill|lt=1973|Q2477|WD=Q2477|1=1973}} ({{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 48). The shiribukagashi grove is also selected as one of the {{ill|lt=100 Green Sites of Osaka|Q11439936|WD=Q11439936|1=Ōsaka Midori no Hyakusen}}{{Cite web|和書|url= http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/midorikikaku/toshiryokka/hyakusenn.html|title=100 Green Sites of Osaka|publisher=Osaka Prefecture |accessdate=2016-12-23}}. === Precincts === * Honden - rebuilt in the {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} period. * Heiden - rebuilt in {{ill|lt=1972|Q2476|WD=Q2476|1=1972}} ({{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 47). * Haiden - rebuilt in 1972 (Shōwa 47). * Shrine office * Bishūkan === Subsidiary shrines === * Kotohira-sha * Hakuryū Tennō-sha === Cultural properties === === Osaka Prefecture designated natural monument === * Mitami Shrine {{ill|lt=Shiribukagashi|Q149476|WD=Q149476|lt=shiribukagashi|1=Lithocarpus glaber}} shrine grove === Location === * Kamotani-dai 1-49-1, Minami Ward, Sakai, Osaka Prefecture === Access === * {{ill|lt=Nankai Senboku Line|Q2758012|WD=Q2758012|1=Semboku Line}} {{ill|lt=Kōmyōike Station|Q6455215|WD=Q6455215|1=Kōmyōike Station}} - 20 minutes on foot === Footnotes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} === Notes === {{Notelist2}} === Sources === {{Reflist|2}} === Related items === * {{ill|lt=Izumi Wada clan|Q65252203|WD=Q65252203|1=Izumi Wada clan}} === External links === {{commons|Category:Mitami-jinja}} *[http://mitami-shrine.com/ Mitami Shrine] *[https://www.sakai-tcb.or.jp/spot/detail/205 Mitami Shrine - Sakai Tourism Guide] {{大阪みどりの百選}} {{神道 横}} {{Shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Mitami Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Mitami Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Mitami Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha in Izumi Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Sakai, Osaka]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izumi Province]] [[Category:Son-sha in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:堺市南区の歴史]] [[Category:堺市南区の建築物]] [[Category:泉北ニュータウン]] [[Category:Natural Monuments Designated by Osaka Prefecture]]

[[File:Mitami-jinja2.jpg|270px]]
Torii and haiden | 所在地 = {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|qid=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Sakai|Q193428|qid=Q193428|1=Sakai}}{{ill|lt=Minami Ward (Sakai)|Q1041996|qid=Q1041996|lt=Minami Ward|1=Minami-ku, Sakai}}Kamotani-dai 1-49-1 | 位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|28|30|N|135|29|08|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | 祭神 = {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|Q2564508|qid=Q2564508|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Koyane}} | 社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}} (small)
former {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|qid=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} | 創建 = Unknown | 本殿 = {{ill|lt=irimoya-zukuri|Q855025|qid=Q855025|1=East Asian hip-and-gable roof}} | 例祭 = First Sunday of October | 神事 = {{ill|lt=yabusame|Q1886157|qid=Q1886157|1=Yabusame}} festival | 地図 = {{Location map|Japan Osaka|lat_deg=34|lat_min=28|lat_sec=30|lon_deg=135|lon_min=29|lon_sec=8|label=Mitami Shrine|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|28|30|N|135|29|08|E}}}} }} '''Mitami Shrine''' (みたみじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Minami Ward (Sakai)|Q1041996|qid=Q1041996|lt=Minami Ward|1=Minami-ku, Sakai}}, {{ill|lt=Sakai|Q193428|qid=Q193428|1=Sakai}}, {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|qid=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}}, and under the former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|qid=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} system it was a {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|qid=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. The grove of {{ill|lt=Shiribukagashi|Q149476|qid=Q149476|1=Lithocarpus glaber}} in the precincts is selected as one of the {{ill|lt=100 Green Sites of Osaka|Q11439936|qid=Q11439936|1=Ōsaka Midori no Hyakusen}}. === Enshrined deities === * Principal deity - {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|Q2564508|qid=Q2564508|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Koyane}} * Jointly enshrined deities - {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|qid=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|qid=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi|Q10936701|qid=Q10936701|1=Ōmononushi}}-no-Mikoto, {{ill|lt=Mikumari deity|Q11129547|qid=Q11129547|lt=Ame-no-Mikumari-no-Ōkami|1=Mikumari}}, {{ill|lt=Ichikishimahime|Q11059454|qid=Q11059454|qid=Q11059454|lt=Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|1=Ichikishima-hime}}, {{ill|lt=Emperor Ojin|Q317997|qid=Q317997|lt=Homuda-wake-no-Mikoto|1=Emperor Ōjin}}, {{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|qid=Q382005|1=Sugawara no Michizane}}, {{ill|lt=Izanagi|Q813858|qid=Q813858|lt=Izanagi-no-Ōkami|1=Izanagi}}, {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|qid=Q276944|1=Ōkuninushi}}-no-Mikoto === History === The exact founding date is unknown, but the name "Mitami Shrine" appears in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|qid=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} completed in {{ill|lt=Engi|Q1327274|qid=Q1327274|lt=Engi|1=Enchō}} 5 ({{ill|lt=927|Q30470|qid=Q30470|1=927}})[https://www.mitami-shrine.com/about/ Mitami Shrine homepage: About Mitami Shrine]. The origin of the shrine name is said to come from "Mitami no Atai" recorded in ''Shinsen Shōjiroku'' and the "Kamiwake entry of Izumi Province." It states that the Mitami no Atai clan were descendants of {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|Q2564508|qid=Q2564508|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Koyane}}, and that the reading of "mitami" is corrected as "mitami" written 美多彌. In the era of {{ill|lt=Emperor Go-Daigo|Q313942|qid=Q313942|1=Emperor Go-Daigo}} during the {{ill|lt=Nanboku-chō period|Q264012|qid=Q264012|1=Nanboku-chō period}}, the shrine is said to have had an extensive precinct of 30,000 tsubo and magnificent buildings. It was also revered as a guardian deity by {{ill|lt=Kusunoki Masashige|Q382234|qid=Q382234|1=Kusunoki Masashige}}. In the 1500s, the shrine-temple Tokusen-ji ({{ill|lt=jingūji|Q1074971|qid=Q1074971|1=Jingū-ji}}) was built in the precincts, and shrine priests and temple clerics managed shrine affairs. It is also said to have been protected by {{ill|lt=Oda Nobunaga|Q171411|qid=Q171411|1=Oda Nobunaga}}, and thus adopted the Oda clan crest (five-leaf mokkō, {{ill|lt=mokkō crest|Q11519285|qid=Q11519285|1=Mokkō mon}}) as its shrine crestMitami Shrine history text. However, in {{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|qid=Q1145058|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} 5 ({{ill|lt=1577|Q6704|qid=Q6704|1=1577}}), it was burned in war during Nobunaga's attack on Saika in Kii ({{ill|lt=Kishū campaign|Q10855721|qid=Q10855721|1=Battle of Kishuu}}), together with nearby {{ill|lt=Hōkō-ji (Sakai)|Q65269233|qid=Q65269233|lt=Hōkō-ji|1=Hōkō-ji}}. In {{ill|lt=Bunroku|Q1373039|qid=Q1373039|1=Bunroku}} 1 ({{ill|lt=1592|Q6769|qid=Q6769|1=1592}}), the honden was rebuilt by {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Wada Dōsan|1=UNKNOWN}} of the {{ill|lt=Kusunoki clan|Q11541208|qid=Q11541208|1=Kusunoki clan}}, and the shrine name was changed to Gozu Tennō-sha[https://www.mitami-shrine.com/yabusame/ Mitami Shrine homepage: About Yabusame]. During the {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|qid=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} separation of Shinto and Buddhism ({{ill|lt=shinbutsu bunri|Q1970535|qid=Q1970535|1=Shinbutsu bunri}}), Tokusen-ji was abolished and the shrine renamed Yasaka Shrine. It was ranked as a {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|qid=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. In {{ill|lt=1907|Q2048|qid=Q2048|1=1907}} (Meiji 40), five nearby village shrines and three unranked shrines were merged, and it was renamed Mitami Shrine as listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|qid=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}. On March 29, {{ill|lt=2014|Q1999|qid=Q1999|1=2014}} ({{ill|lt=Heisei|Q234510|qid=Q234510|1=Heisei era}} 26), the {{ill|lt=yabusame|Q1886157|qid=Q1886157|1=Yabusame}} rite was revived. The shrine grove covers 8,250 {{ill|lt=square meters|Q25343|qid=Q25343|lt=m2|1=Square metre}} and includes evergreen broadleaf trees such as {{ill|lt=Shiribukagashi|Q149476|qid=Q149476|1=Lithocarpus glaber}}, {{ill|lt=Arakashi|Q714124|qid=Q714124|1=Quercus glauca}}, {{ill|lt=sakaki|Q775470|qid=Q775470|1=Cleyera japonica}}, and camphor. Shiribukagashi in particular rarely forms a forest; because this grove lies near the eastern limit of its distribution, it was designated an Osaka Prefecture {{ill|lt=natural monument|Q23790|qid=Q23790|1=Natural monument}} in {{ill|lt=1973|Q2477|qid=Q2477|1=1973}} ({{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|qid=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 48). The shiribukagashi grove is also selected as one of the {{ill|lt=100 Green Sites of Osaka|Q11439936|qid=Q11439936|1=Ōsaka Midori no Hyakusen}}{{Cite web|和書|url= http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/midorikikaku/toshiryokka/hyakusenn.html|title=100 Green Sites of Osaka|publisher=Osaka Prefecture |accessdate=2016-12-23}}. === Precincts === * Honden - rebuilt in the {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|qid=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} period. * Heiden - rebuilt in {{ill|lt=1972|Q2476|qid=Q2476|1=1972}} ({{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|qid=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 47). * Haiden - rebuilt in 1972 (Shōwa 47). * Shrine office * Bishūkan === Subsidiary shrines === * Kotohira-sha * Hakuryū Tennō-sha === Cultural properties === === Osaka Prefecture designated natural monument === * Mitami Shrine {{ill|lt=Shiribukagashi|Q149476|qid=Q149476|lt=shiribukagashi|1=Lithocarpus glaber}} shrine grove === Location === * Kamotani-dai 1-49-1, Minami Ward, Sakai, Osaka Prefecture === Access === * {{ill|lt=Nankai Senboku Line|Q2758012|qid=Q2758012|1=Semboku Line}} {{ill|lt=Kōmyōike Station|Q6455215|qid=Q6455215|1=Kōmyōike Station}} - 20 minutes on foot === Footnotes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} === Notes === {{Notelist2}} === Sources === {{Reflist|2}} === Related items === * {{ill|lt=Izumi Wada clan|Q65252203|qid=Q65252203|1=Izumi Wada clan}} === External links === {{commons|Category:Mitami-jinja}} *[http://mitami-shrine.com/ Mitami Shrine] *[https://www.sakai-tcb.or.jp/spot/detail/205 Mitami Shrine - Sakai Tourism Guide] {{大阪みどりの百選}} {{神道 横}} {{Shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Mitami Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Mitami Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Mitami Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha in Izumi Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Sakai, Osaka]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izumi Province]] [[Category:Son-sha in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:堺市南区の歴史]] [[Category:堺市南区の建築物]] [[Category:泉北ニュータウン]] [[Category:Natural Monuments Designated by Osaka Prefecture]]

former {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|qid=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} | 創建 = Unknown | 本殿 = {{ill|lt=irimoya-zukuri|Q855025|qid=Q855025|1=East Asian hip-and-gable roof}} | 例祭 = First Sunday of October | 神事 = {{ill|lt=yabusame|Q1886157|qid=Q1886157|1=Yabusame}} festival | 地図 = {{Location map|Japan Osaka|lat_deg=34|lat_min=28|lat_sec=30|lon_deg=135|lon_min=29|lon_sec=8|label=Mitami Shrine|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|28|30|N|135|29|08|E}}}} }} '''Mitami Shrine''' (みたみじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Minami Ward (Sakai)|Q1041996|qid=Q1041996|lt=Minami Ward|1=Minami-ku, Sakai}}, {{ill|lt=Sakai|Q193428|qid=Q193428|1=Sakai}}, {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|qid=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}}, and under the former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|qid=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} system it was a {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|qid=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. The grove of {{ill|lt=Shiribukagashi|Q149476|qid=Q149476|1=Lithocarpus glaber}} in the precincts is selected as one of the {{ill|lt=100 Green Sites of Osaka|Q11439936|qid=Q11439936|1=Ōsaka Midori no Hyakusen}}. === Enshrined deities === * Principal deity - {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|Q2564508|qid=Q2564508|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Koyane}} * Jointly enshrined deities - {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|qid=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|qid=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi|Q10936701|qid=Q10936701|1=Ōmononushi}}-no-Mikoto, {{ill|lt=Mikumari deity|Q11129547|qid=Q11129547|lt=Ame-no-Mikumari-no-Ōkami|1=Mikumari}}, {{ill|lt=Ichikishimahime|Q11059454|qid=Q11059454|qid=Q11059454|lt=Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|1=Ichikishima-hime}}, {{ill|lt=Emperor Ojin|Q317997|qid=Q317997|lt=Homuda-wake-no-Mikoto|1=Emperor Ōjin}}, {{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|qid=Q382005|1=Sugawara no Michizane}}, {{ill|lt=Izanagi|Q813858|qid=Q813858|lt=Izanagi-no-Ōkami|1=Izanagi}}, {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|qid=Q276944|1=Ōkuninushi}}-no-Mikoto === History === The exact founding date is unknown, but the name "Mitami Shrine" appears in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|qid=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} completed in {{ill|lt=Engi|Q1327274|qid=Q1327274|lt=Engi|1=Enchō}} 5 ({{ill|lt=927|Q30470|qid=Q30470|1=927}})[https://www.mitami-shrine.com/about/ Mitami Shrine homepage: About Mitami Shrine]. The origin of the shrine name is said to come from "Mitami no Atai" recorded in ''Shinsen Shōjiroku'' and the "Kamiwake entry of Izumi Province." It states that the Mitami no Atai clan were descendants of {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|Q2564508|qid=Q2564508|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Koyane}}, and that the reading of "mitami" is corrected as "mitami" written 美多彌. In the era of {{ill|lt=Emperor Go-Daigo|Q313942|qid=Q313942|1=Emperor Go-Daigo}} during the {{ill|lt=Nanboku-chō period|Q264012|qid=Q264012|1=Nanboku-chō period}}, the shrine is said to have had an extensive precinct of 30,000 tsubo and magnificent buildings. It was also revered as a guardian deity by {{ill|lt=Kusunoki Masashige|Q382234|qid=Q382234|1=Kusunoki Masashige}}. In the 1500s, the shrine-temple Tokusen-ji ({{ill|lt=jingūji|Q1074971|qid=Q1074971|1=Jingū-ji}}) was built in the precincts, and shrine priests and temple clerics managed shrine affairs. It is also said to have been protected by {{ill|lt=Oda Nobunaga|Q171411|qid=Q171411|1=Oda Nobunaga}}, and thus adopted the Oda clan crest (five-leaf mokkō, {{ill|lt=mokkō crest|Q11519285|qid=Q11519285|1=Mokkō mon}}) as its shrine crestMitami Shrine history text. However, in {{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|qid=Q1145058|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} 5 ({{ill|lt=1577|Q6704|qid=Q6704|1=1577}}), it was burned in war during Nobunaga's attack on Saika in Kii ({{ill|lt=Kishū campaign|Q10855721|qid=Q10855721|1=Battle of Kishuu}}), together with nearby {{ill|lt=Hōkō-ji (Sakai)|Q65269233|qid=Q65269233|lt=Hōkō-ji|1=Hōkō-ji}}. In {{ill|lt=Bunroku|Q1373039|qid=Q1373039|1=Bunroku}} 1 ({{ill|lt=1592|Q6769|qid=Q6769|1=1592}}), the honden was rebuilt by {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Wada Dōsan|1=UNKNOWN}} of the {{ill|lt=Kusunoki clan|Q11541208|qid=Q11541208|1=Kusunoki clan}}, and the shrine name was changed to Gozu Tennō-sha[https://www.mitami-shrine.com/yabusame/ Mitami Shrine homepage: About Yabusame]. During the {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|qid=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} separation of Shinto and Buddhism ({{ill|lt=shinbutsu bunri|Q1970535|qid=Q1970535|1=Shinbutsu bunri}}), Tokusen-ji was abolished and the shrine renamed Yasaka Shrine. It was ranked as a {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|qid=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. In {{ill|lt=1907|Q2048|qid=Q2048|1=1907}} (Meiji 40), five nearby village shrines and three unranked shrines were merged, and it was renamed Mitami Shrine as listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|qid=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}. On March 29, {{ill|lt=2014|Q1999|qid=Q1999|1=2014}} ({{ill|lt=Heisei|Q234510|qid=Q234510|1=Heisei era}} 26), the {{ill|lt=yabusame|Q1886157|qid=Q1886157|1=Yabusame}} rite was revived. The shrine grove covers 8,250 {{ill|lt=square meters|Q25343|qid=Q25343|lt=m2|1=Square metre}} and includes evergreen broadleaf trees such as {{ill|lt=Shiribukagashi|Q149476|qid=Q149476|1=Lithocarpus glaber}}, {{ill|lt=Arakashi|Q714124|qid=Q714124|1=Quercus glauca}}, {{ill|lt=sakaki|Q775470|qid=Q775470|1=Cleyera japonica}}, and camphor. Shiribukagashi in particular rarely forms a forest; because this grove lies near the eastern limit of its distribution, it was designated an Osaka Prefecture {{ill|lt=natural monument|Q23790|qid=Q23790|1=Natural monument}} in {{ill|lt=1973|Q2477|qid=Q2477|1=1973}} ({{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|qid=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 48). The shiribukagashi grove is also selected as one of the {{ill|lt=100 Green Sites of Osaka|Q11439936|qid=Q11439936|1=Ōsaka Midori no Hyakusen}}{{Cite web|和書|url= http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/midorikikaku/toshiryokka/hyakusenn.html|title=100 Green Sites of Osaka|publisher=Osaka Prefecture |accessdate=2016-12-23}}. === Precincts === * Honden - rebuilt in the {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|qid=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} period. * Heiden - rebuilt in {{ill|lt=1972|Q2476|qid=Q2476|1=1972}} ({{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|qid=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 47). * Haiden - rebuilt in 1972 (Shōwa 47). * Shrine office * Bishūkan === Subsidiary shrines === * Kotohira-sha * Hakuryū Tennō-sha === Cultural properties === === Osaka Prefecture designated natural monument === * Mitami Shrine {{ill|lt=Shiribukagashi|Q149476|qid=Q149476|lt=shiribukagashi|1=Lithocarpus glaber}} shrine grove === Location === * Kamotani-dai 1-49-1, Minami Ward, Sakai, Osaka Prefecture === Access === * {{ill|lt=Nankai Senboku Line|Q2758012|qid=Q2758012|1=Semboku Line}} {{ill|lt=Kōmyōike Station|Q6455215|qid=Q6455215|1=Kōmyōike Station}} - 20 minutes on foot === Footnotes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} === Notes === {{Notelist2}} === Sources === {{Reflist|2}} === Related items === * {{ill|lt=Izumi Wada clan|Q65252203|qid=Q65252203|1=Izumi Wada clan}} === External links === {{commons|Category:Mitami-jinja}} *[http://mitami-shrine.com/ Mitami Shrine] *[https://www.sakai-tcb.or.jp/spot/detail/205 Mitami Shrine - Sakai Tourism Guide] {{大阪みどりの百選}} {{神道 横}} {{Shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Mitami Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Mitami Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Mitami Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha in Izumi Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Sakai, Osaka]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izumi Province]] [[Category:Son-sha in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:堺市南区の歴史]] [[Category:堺市南区の建築物]] [[Category:泉北ニュータウン]] [[Category:Natural Monuments Designated by Osaka Prefecture]]
